name: Ascent to Almenara from Riópar — circular
name_original: Subida al Almenara desde Riópar — circular
region_intro: Jogging around Riópar offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra del Segura, characterized by mountainous terrain and extensive trail networks. The region is largely protected by the Calares del Mundo and Sima Natural Park, providing a pristine environment with varied ecosystems. Runners can expect significant elevation changes, pine groves, and routes along river valleys, including the source of the Mundo River. This area provides a range of running experiences, from challenging ascents to scenic paths. Best jogging routes around Riópar The most popular jogging route is Ascent to Almenara from Riópar — circular, a 13.1 miles (21.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant ascent to Almenara, offering panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local runners is El Gollizo, Pino Gordo del Toril y Pico del Gallina — circular desde Riópar, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This route winds through pine groves and includes challenging ascents to peaks like Pico del Gallina. Local runners also love the Riópar and Nacimiento del Río Mundo Loop, a 11.3 miles (18.1 km) trail leading through the natural park to the spectacular source of the Mundo River, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Jogging around Riópar is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation gains. The routes in Riópar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 380 runners have used komoot to explore Riópar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Riópar?
A: Riópar offers over 20 dedicated running routes, providing a wide range of options for exploring the Sierra del Segura and the Calares del Mundo and Sima Natural Park. These routes vary in length and difficulty, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Riópar?
A: Jogging routes in Riópar feature diverse terrain, from pine groves and river valleys to significant elevation changes within the mountainous Sierra del Segura. You'll encounter paths with varying surfaces, including those leading to spectacular waterfalls and panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the Riópar area?
A: While many routes in Riópar are considered moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain and elevation gains, there are some less strenuous options. It's advisable to check the route profiles on komoot for specific details on distance and elevation to find a path that suits your fitness level.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Riópar?
A: Yes, Riópar offers several excellent circular running routes. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1081127294/ascent-to-almenara-from-riopar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Almenara from Riópar — circular&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1081126489/el-gollizo-pino-gordo-del-toril-y-pico-del-gallina-circular-desde-riopar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gollizo, Pino Gordo del Toril y Pico del Gallina — circular desde Riópar&lt;/a&gt; winds through beautiful pine groves.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls or natural monuments?
A: Absolutely! A highlight for many runners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1081123850/riopar-and-nacimiento-del-rio-mundo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar and Nacimiento del Río Mundo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which leads directly to the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt; and its impressive waterfalls. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saltaor Waterfall (Zurriero)&lt;/a&gt; on other trails.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while running in Riópar?
A: Many routes offer stunning views. You can incorporate a visit to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, the old town perched on a hill, for charming vistas. Trails also lead to high points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Landscapes Around Riópar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Riópar suitable for advanced runners looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, Riópar is an excellent destination for advanced runners. The region's mountainous terrain, particularly within the Sierra del Segura, means many routes feature significant elevation gains and rugged paths. Over 15 routes are classified as difficult, providing a substantial physical challenge.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Riópar?
A: The running routes in Riópar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the pristine environment of the Calares del Mundo and Sima Natural Park, the varied ecosystems, and the rewarding views from challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options available?
A: For those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Luis Escudero, Riópar – Riópar Viejo loop from Riópar&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 60 km, offering an extensive exploration of the region's landscapes and cultural points. These longer routes typically involve significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Riópar?
A: Riópar, with its natural park setting, offers pleasant conditions for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures and lush scenery, ideal for outdoor activities. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended, while winters offer a different, often tranquil, experience.

Q: Are there any running routes that follow the GR 66 trail?
A: Yes, you can experience sections of the GR 66 trail while running in Riópar. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 66 markers – Visitor center loop from Riópar&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that incorporates parts of this long-distance path, offering impressive vistas from higher altitudes.
tips: Riópar Viejo is a small town in the heart of the natural park of Calares del Mundo and Sima. It was abandoned at the beginning of the 20th century due to the emigration of its inhabitants to other places in search of work. Today, it is a popular tourist spot due to its rustic and secluded atmosphere, and the surviving ruins and ancient structures, such as the 16th-century church, castle remains, and old stone houses.

Almenara Peak is a 1796 meter high mountain in the Alcaraz mountain range. It is important for its visibility from many kilometers and for being the highest point of this mountain range.

It is not a mountain that is difficult to access on any of its sides, but the normal ascent route is on its south face from the source of Pino de los Muchachos.

Riópar is located in the Sierra de Alcaraz and has an impressive natural environment. In the town, it is worth seeing the Royal Factories of San Juan de Alcaraz, the Paseo de los Plátanos, the Plaza de Luiz Escudero, the Molino reservoir and the old town.
